videos.title: Never Buy Bread Again! The Easiest 4-Ingredient Daily Bread (No Kneading)
videos.description:
Make bakery-quality bread at home with just 4 ingredients and no kneading. This recipe builds incredible flavor through a simple preferment, and the stretch-and-fold method does all the work for you. If you've ever struggled with bread, this one will change that.

*Ingredients:*

*Starter:*
* Water - ½ cup (120 ml)
* Fresh yeast - ¾ tsp. (3 g) or dry yeast - ½ tsp. (2 g)
* Bread flour - 1 cup (120 g)

*Main dough:*
* Water - 7 tbsp (100 ml)
* Bread flour - 1⅔ cups (200 g)
* Salt - 1 tsp (6 g)

This is really it! 4 basic ingredients to make amazing, bakery-quality bread.

*My other awesome channels:*
Sweet Bread Recipes 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@sweet-bread-recipes
Cookie Corner 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@The-Cookie-Corner

Tools I Used:

Baking pan I used to bake this bread: https://amzn.to/3N8ZPUi (I used two of them, one turned upside down and used as cover)

Alternative pan:
* https://amzn.to/49UyaOo - either two of these, one turned upside down and used as a cover, or one, but covered with aluminum foil for the first bake.
* https://amzn.to/4gnx6Fb - same as the previous pan.

* https://amzn.to/4qCUXVM - my favorite bread lame

You get two loaves from one batch, each with a thin, flaky crust that crackles as it cools and a soft, open crumb inside. They slice cleanly and taste just as good the next day.

If you try this recipe, leave a comment below and let me know how it turned out! Subscribe for a new bread recipe every week.

Disclaimer: As an Amazon Associate, I earn from qualifying purchases.

#bread #foodlanguage #recipe

videos.title: I don’t buy bread anymore! This yogurt bread changes everything
videos.description:
If you're tired of buying store-bought bread, this easy braided yogurt bread is the perfect homemade solution. With just a few basic ingredients and no fancy equipment, you'll get bakery-quality bread with a thin, crispy crust and a soft, fluffy interior. The simple stretch-and-fold technique makes it incredibly easy, even if you've never made bread before.

*Ingredients:*

* Water - ⅔ cup (160 ml)
* Plain yogurt - ¼ cup (60 g)
* Fresh yeast - 1 tsp. (3 g) or dry yeast - ½ tsp. (2 g)
* Bread flour - 1 ½ cups (220 g)
* Whole wheat flour - ¾ cup (100 g)
* Salt - 1 tsp (6 g)

*My other awesome channels:*
Cookie Corner 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@The-Cookie-Corner
Sweet Bread Recipes 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@sweet-bread-recipes

Tools I Used:

* https://amzn.to/3MZFlNU - loaf pan
* https://amzn.to/3LyRkBx - another loaf pan
* https://amzn.to/3MZOuGh - another loaf pan

* https://amzn.to/4qCUXVM - my favorite bread lame

The result is a beautifully braided loaf with a super thin, crispy crust and an incredibly soft, cloud-like interior. The yogurt adds a subtle tang and creates an amazing texture that stays fresh for days. Perfect for slicing and serving with butter, or using for sandwiches. The braid looks impressive but is surprisingly easy to master.

I hope you love this recipe! Don't forget to subscribe for more easy bread recipes and give this video a thumbs up if you enjoyed it.

Disclaimer: As an Amazon Associate, I earn from qualifying purchases.

#bread #foodlanguage #recipe

videos.title: I don't buy bread anymore! The new easy French baguettes recipe
videos.description:
Homemade baguettes with a thin, crackly crust and an open, airy crumb - and they're actually easy to make. This recipe uses a biga preferment that takes just a few minutes to prepare the night before, and the result is bakery-quality bread at home with minimal effort.

*Ingredients:*

*Biga (preferment):*
* Bread flour - ¾ cup (100 g)
* Fresh yeast - ⅛ tsp. (0.5 g) or dry yeast - ⅛ tsp. (0.5 g)
* Water - 3 tbsp. (50 ml)

*Main dough:*
* Water - 1 cup (230 ml)
* Bread flour - 2 ½ cups (320 g)
* Salt - 1¼ tsp. (7 g)

This is really it! 3 basic ingredients to make classic homemade baguettes with a proper crumb and a crust that sings when it cools down.

*My other awesome channels:*
Sweet Bread Recipes 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@sweet-bread-recipes
Cookie Corner 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@The-Cookie-Corner

Tools I Used:

Baking pan I used to bake this bread: https://amzn.to/3N8ZPUi (I used two of them, one turned upside down and used as cover)

Alternative pan:
* https://amzn.to/49UyaOo - either two of these, one turned upside down and used as a cover, or one, but covered with aluminum foil for the first bake.
* https://amzn.to/4gnx6Fb - same as the previous pan.

* https://amzn.to/4qCUXVM - my favorite bread lame

These baguettes come out with a crispy, crackly crust and a soft, open crumb inside. They're easy to slice, great for sandwiches, and even better just torn apart fresh out of the oven. No bread machine, no special equipment - just a baking tray and a sharp blade.

If you enjoyed this recipe, hit Like and Subscribe for more easy homemade bread recipes every week!

videos.title: How to Bake the Best Focaccia Bread You've Ever Tasted
videos.description:
Making focaccia bread at home is magical. The smells, the thrill of waiting for the dough to rise, the gentle dimpling of the dough before sending it to bake, your face stuck to the oven's window and the anticipation. And then, the moment of truth, when you cut a slice and hear the crunch, and see the airy texture and the moist crumb. And finally, the taste, the heavenly taste of fresh bread, olive oil, and history. Trust me, you will fall in love with this focaccia bread.

I will show you how to bake the best focaccia bread you've ever tasted. If you follow my recipe, you will feel like a chef, your focaccia bread will impress anyone, yourself included. This is a NO KNEAD recipe. That's right, perfect result without even the need to... knead. It's also a same day focaccia bread, it will be ready in less than 2 hours, prep and proofing included.

To make this no knead focaccia bread, you will need the following ingredients:

•  Bread flour - 4 cups (500 grams)
•  Water - 1 ⅔ cup (400 milliliters)
•  Brewer's yeast - 4 tsp. (12 grams) or dry yeast - 2 tsp. (6 grams)
•  Sugar - 1 tsp.
•  Olive oil - 2 tbsp. plus more for greasing and drizzling
•  Salt - 2 tsp. plus more for sprinkling

Full printable recipe here 👉 https://food-language.com/recipes/the-best-focaccia-bread-ever/

*My other awesome channels:*
Sweet Bread Recipes 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@sweet-bread-recipes
Cookie Corner 👉 https://www.youtube.com/@The-Cookie-Corner

Tools I Used:
* https://amzn.to/3N8ZPUi - baking pan
* https://amzn.to/49UyaOo -  another baking pan.
* https://amzn.to/4gnx6Fb - same as the previous pan.

The process is very simple and fun, and you don't need any special equipment or skills. You just need to mix the ingredients, let the dough rest and rise, make a couple of simple stretches (they really take less than a minute), spread it on a baking tray, dimple it with your fingers, sprinkle some water and salt, and bake it in a hot oven. That's it!

For the detailed instructions, please watch the video and follow along. You will see how easy and enjoyable it is to make this focaccia bread.

Here is the full transcript of the video:

Water - 1 ⅔ cup (400 milliliters)
Brewer's yeast - 4 tsp. (12 grams) or dry yeast - 2 tsp. (6 grams)
Crumble into the water
Sugar - 1 tsp.
Mix until the yeast is dissolved
Bread flour - 4 cups (500 grams)
Add the water and yeast mixture
Mix
The dough is soft and easy to mix with a wooden spoon
Olive oil - 2 tbsp.
Focaccia loooooves olive oil
Salt - 2 tsp.
Mix to incorporate the olive oil
That's it! You don't need to... knead
Clean the spoon and the bowl sides
Cover and let rest for 30 minutes
Soak your hand in clean water
Grab the 4 sides of the dough and fold them to the center
Soak your hand before each fold
This stretching will help develop the gluten network
Lift the dough from the center and slap it into the bowl a couple of times
Cover and let rest for 30 minutes
Already full of bubbles!
So wobbly!
Let's do the same process as before one more time
Stretch and fold the 4 sides
Lift and slap a couple of times
It's ready now!
Grease the tray with olive oil
Be generous, focaccia loves olive oil!
Transfer the dough into the tray
More olive oil? Yes, trust me!
A relaxing olive oil massage
The goal now is to spread the dough, don't worry if it doesn't cover the entire tray
Cover with another tray and let rest for 30 minutes
Guess what?
Guess what?
Just a little more olive oil
Dimple the focaccia with your fingers
Dip the fingers until you feel the tray, you don't have to be very gentle
Sprinkle a little water on top
Sprinkle a little coarse salt
Bake at 440°F (230°C) for 20 minutes
Look at it! Wait until you see the bubbles!
Transfer to a wire rack to cool (if you have the patience)
Now let's hear some music
Full of bubbles
Light like a feather
Wait to see the inside!
There's crust underneath too!
Moment of truth
Perfect!
Click the like button if you like focaccia!

I hope you will give this recipe a try and let me know what you think. If you like this video, please give it a thumbs up and subscribe to my channel for more baking and cooking videos. Thank you for watching and happy baking!

Disclaimer: As an Amazon Associate, I earn from qualifying purchases.

Deluxe Hot Chocolate with Homemade Marshmallow Fluff | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
I strongly believe that there is no greater hot beverage than a really good hot chocolate, once the temperature starts to dip. But you don't need to buy the weird powder from the supermarket because you can make your very own, to have on hand whenever you need to whip one up after a chilly outing! And, since it's the festive period, I thought I'd make it extra special by adding a marshmallow fluff top. Merry Christmas one and all!

Ingredients
Hot Chocolate Powder
100g good quality dark chocolate (50-70%)
60g cocoa powder
40g toasted milk powder (optional)
20-40g rapadura sugar (or light brown or muscovado is also great!)
Seeds from up to 1 pod of vanilla
pinch sea salt

Homemade Marshmallow Fluff
145g golden syrup (any invert sugar syrup will work)
50g water
85g caster sufgar
60g egg whites
1/3 tsp cream of tartar
vanilla seeds or 1/2 tsp extract
pinch sea salt


-----------

videos.title: Blackberry & White Chocolate Crumble Cupcake Recipe & Tutorial | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
It's most definitely the season for all things delicious and warming and cosy, so please let me introduce you to these gorgeous Blackberry & White Chocolate Crumble Cupcakes. 

Sponges rippled and filled with blackberry goo, topped with the silkiest white chocolate swiss meringue buttercream and finished with a crunchy, oaty crumble topping - it's quite literally the best of all the textures & flavours! 

I hope you enjoy this recipe and make them! If you do, please share your pictures over on instagram using #cupcakejemma so we can see how you get on xx

CUPCAKES
125g Butter
125g Caster Sugar 
2 Eggs 
135g SR Flour 
1.5 tbsp Milk 
Blackberry Goo Swirl 

BLACKBERRY GOO
400g Blackberries 
125g Caster Sugar 

BUTTERCREAM
3 Large Egg Whites 
225g Caster Sugar 
300g Soft Butter
120g White Choc (melted)

OAT CRUMBLE
80g Plain Flour 
30g Oats 
10g Caster Sugar 
10g Demerara Sugar 
40g Melted Butter 
Pinch of Salt

Soft, Squishy STRAWBERRIES & CREAM MOCHI Recipe | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
If you love a curious chew, or an unusual bounce in your sweets then this recipe is going to be right up your street. We're clinging onto Summer and using the last of the strawberries (though, you can use frozen ones too) to create these little pillows of fluffy, creamy, fruity fun! Are you a Mochi fan? Let us know in the comments! 

(Shout out to Jessica from  ⁨@u-taste⁩  for inspiring this recipe ❤️🍓. Please go and check out her original recipes)

Ingredients
Strawberry Gel
6g powdered gelatine + 4 tbsp water
340g fresh or frozen strawberries
55g caster sugar
1 tbsp lemon juice

Mochi Dough
105g glutinous rice flour
30g cornflour/cornstarch
40g caster sugar
175g whole milk
25g unsalted butter
1/2 tsp vanilla extract
pinch sea salt

Strawberry Cream filling
250g double/heavy cream
3-6 tbsp strawberry gel

videos.title: Peanut Butter Pie with Cinnamon Cereal Crust Recipe | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
For this episode of Cupcake Jemma Cooks The Books, I'm diving headlong into the most useful recipe book of the modern age! Flavour Heroes by Gurdeep Loyal is designed to help you use up those modern store cupboard ingredients, such as tamarind, maple syrup and, in the case of this recipe, peanut butter, in new resourceful and exciting ways! This Peanut Butter Pie with Cinnamon Cereal Crust is going to be the star of the show at my next dinner party! You have to try it! 

Ingredients
For the Pastry Cream filling
6 lg egg yolks
125g caster sugar
65g cornflour
2.5 tsp powdered gelatine
500g whole milk
2 tsp vanilla extract
150g very soft butter
275g dark roast peanut butter

For the Crust
80g butter, melted (for the cornflakes)
80g soft light brown sugar
2 tsp ground cinnamon
265g crushed chocolate sandwich cookies
60g butter, melted (to bind the crumbs)
pinch of salt

Extra
150-200g mascarpone cream
150-200g double cream
(equal amounts of each)
Dark roasted peanuts

-----------

Cereal Milk Tarts Recipe & Tutorial | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
The duo you've all missed are back after doing a very exciting project (I'm sure you know what that is by now!). Sally & Dane have learnt a lot of new skills over the past few months and are so excited to share them with you all - starting with these gorgeous Cereal Milk Tarts! They are SO delicious and a bit of a project, so if you're up for the challenge, grab a pen and paper because we're heading back to class to learn from the professionals 😉

xx

PASTRY 
135g Cold Butter
95g Icing Sugar
65g Egg Yolk
265g Plain Flour
½ tsp Vanilla
Pinch Salt
EGG WASH - 1 Yolk + 10g Double Cream

CAKE
4 Eggs, Separated 
115g Plain Flour
1 tsp (heaped) Baking Powder
¼ tsp Salt
160g Caster Sugar (Split 120g for the yolks and 40g for the whites)
2.5 tbsp Whole Milk
½ tsp Vanilla

CEREAL MILK
100g Cornflakes
25g Caster Sugar
25g Horlicks
60g Melted Butter
Pinch Of Salt
150g Evaporated Milk
250g Condensed Milk
6 tbsp Whole Milk

STRAWBERRY INSERT
400g Fresh Strawberries
65g Caster Sugar
1g Pectin NH

BROWN SUGAR MERINGUE 
120g Muscovado Sugar
210g Light Brown Sugar
30g Glucose
150ml Water
120g Egg White
½ tsp Vanilla
Pinch of Salt

videos.title: Velvet Volcano Sheet Cake | Cooks The Books | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
I'm cooking THE book today and getting high on my own supply because this week, I'm baking from the Crumbs & Doilies book. But this time, I'm tweaking recipes, scaling recipes down so I can use a different tin, trying things I've never tried before with Swiss Meringue Buttercream, it's WILD! 

You can of course buy the book all all good book retailers or signed copies here - https://cupcakejemma.com/products/the-crumbs-doilies-recipe-book?variant=39788033704054

INGREDIENTS for a 9" x 13" rectangular cake

Red Velvet Sponge
170g buttermilk (or 115g plain yoghurt + 55g water)
1/3 tsp vanilla extract
1 tsp red extra food colour paste
167g soft unsalted butter
400g caster sugar
4 large eggs
250g plain flour
33g cocoa powder
1/3 tsp sea salt
1 tsp apple cider vinegar
2/3 tsp bicarbonate of soda

Cookie Dough
120g soft unsalted butter
100g plain flour (toasted if you like)
70g caster sugar
45g soft light brown sugar
1/2 tsp sea salt
1/2 tsp vanilla extract
2 tbsp yoghurt
50g dark chocolate chips

Salted Caramel (will make way more than you need!)
175g double cream
1/2 tsp vanilla extract
1/2 tsp sea salt
175g caster sugar
75g water

Oreo Swiss Meringue Buttercream
75g egg whites
150g caster sugar
180g soft unsalted butter
pinch sea salt
1/4 tsp vanilla extract
2-6 tbsp finely ground Oreo crumbs (to taste)

Cream Cheese Icing
63g soft unsalted butter
pinch sea salt
100g cream cheese (room temp)
350g icing sugar, sifted

Optional Extra Bits
Chocolate ganache or melted chocolate for drizzling
Oreo cookie pieces for decoration

-----------

videos.title: Easter Chocolate Nest Cupcakes | Tutorial & How To | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
Easter is one of our FAVOURITE times of year to bake at Crumbs & Doilies, and it honestly seems crazy that we haven't shared the recipe for one of our most loved cupcakes.. The Easter Nest Cupcake!! 

Squishy vanilla sponges topped with silky buttercream, splatted with gorgeous cocoa & finished with crispy, crunchy, DELICIOUS chocolate nests, filled with mini eggs! You can find them on the counter all week at C&D Soho or order online for delivery or collection from our bakery! 

HAPPY EASTER! 🐣🌷 xx

MAKES 12 CUPCAKES 

CUPCAKES
125g Self Raising Flour

2 Eggs 
1.5 tbsp Whole Milk 
1/4 tsp Vanilla Extract

Food Colouring of you choice 

COCOA SPLATS 
10g Cocoa Powder
1-3 tbsp Water 

CHOCOLATE NESTS 
200g Dark Chocolate 
50g Butter
50g Golden Syrup
4 Blocks Shredded Wheat 
Mini Eggs to finish

Fluffy Cardamom Bun Recipe & Tutorial | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
Since I visited Chai Guys Bakehouse and tried their Cardamom Buns, I've dreamed of making my own version...Dreams can come true y'all. I've only gone and done it for you. These are surprisingly simple to make and your friends and family are going to LOVE you forever if you bake them (provided you actually share them, of course). They're sticky on the outside and fluffy and fragrant on the inside. Let me know what you think!

Ingredients
For the Tangzhong
65g whole milk
25g strong white bread flour

For the Dough
the above Tangzhong, plus
90g whole milk
1 large egg
240g strong white bread flour
4g dried active yeast
30g sugar
4g sea salt
60g soft unsalted butter
(1 egg for the egg wash)

For the Filling
100g soft unsalted butter
100g soft light brown sugar
1.5 tsp ground cardamom
pinch of sea salt
A Little Lime Zest (optional)

Simple Syrup
50g sugar
50g water
3-4 bashed cardamom pods

----------

videos.title: The Biggest Cake We've Ever Baked?! | Behind The Scenes | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
If you've been with us for a while, you'll know we have a bakery in London called Crumbs & Doilies (shoutout to you if you've been in to visit us!).. We've had our little shop in Soho for 10 years which is kind of crazy - the time has literally flown by! We wanted to mark the occasion in the best way - with free cake! And not just any cake.. the biggest cake we've ever made! 

THE CAKE WAS 
- Chocolate & Caramel Sponges
- Salted Caramel Swiss Meringue Buttercream Inside
- Pools of Salted Caramel Sauce
- Confetti Buttercream Outside
- White Chocolate Drip
- Sweeties to Finish!

Sally, Jemma & the team baked and decorated the cake and we thought you might like to see how we did it! 

We also want to say a massive thank you to everyone that's supported us in store and online over the last 10 years, it honestly means so much to us 🩷
Here's to the next 10! 

xx

videos.title: Making the biggest cake EVER for the 10th birthday of C&D Soho 🩷✨
Malty Figgy Pudding Recipe & Tutorial | Cooks The Books | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
Who doesn't have at least ONE Ottolenghi cookbook on their shelf? Trust me. This is going to be a real crowd pleaser at your Betwixmas dinner! It's the Malty Figgy Pudding from the new Ottolenghi cookbook, Comfort. It's going to warm your belly AND your cockles! Like an internal hug. It's a fantastic and it's becoming a firm favourite of mine. 

Pick up a copy of Comfort for yourself: 

Recipe
150g plain flour
2 tbsp cocoa powder

2 tbsp malted milk powder (such as Horlicks)
50g dark soft brown sugar
50g ground almonds
130g unsalted butter, melted
100g milk

3 tbsp maple syrup
1 egg, lightly beaten
150g soft dried figs, roughly chopped
50g dark chocolate, roughly chopped

Topping
100g dark soft brown sugar
1 tbsp malted milk powder
Pinch of salt
25g unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
200g water
150g double cream

To serve
Creme fraiche, flaked almonds, maple syrup

Banana Tatin Cake Recipe & Tutorial | Cooks The Books | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
This week I'm cooking from Philip Khoury's book, A New Way To Bake. Plant-based baking often gets a bad rap but this book is choc full of incredibly delicious and super simple plant-based recipes from a genuine genius. I chose the Banana Tatin Cake and it was divine! I really hope you like it and, whether you're rocking a plant-based vibe or not, check out Phil's book.

Ingredients
80g muscovado/dark brown soft sugar (1)
5 whole bananas
300g very ripe bananas, mashed
100g muscovado/dark brown sugar (2)
200g plant-based milk
10g vanilla extract/paste
50g extra virgin olive oil
240g plain flour
1 tsp bicarbonate of soda
2 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp sea salt

Give @philkhoury a follow on instagram and please grab a copy of his book for yourself! - https://www.foyles.co.uk/book/a-new-way-to-bake/philip-khoury/9781784885922

videos.title: S’mores eclairs: need I say more? The recipes up on our channel right now.
S'mores Eclairs Recipe & Tutorial | Choux Pastry How To | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
Sally & Dane are back to share the recipe for one of our favourite bakes to date.. the S'mores Eclair! Super light, crispy choux pastry filled with a sweet biscuit creme diplomat and topped with a silky chocolate glaze and finished with toasted italian meringue.. it's honestly as incredible as it sounds! 

There are many elements to these eclairs, so we applaud you if you make them all - don't forget to tag us in your snaps over on instagram when you recreate these and use the hashtag #cupcakejemma! 

Happy Baking! x 

CHOUX PASTRY (makes approx 16)
250ml Cold Water 
1.5 tsp Sugar 
1/4 tsp Sea Salt 
115g Unsalted Butter 
135g Plain Flour (All Purpose)
4-5 Eggs

BISCUIT CREME DIPLOMAT

Easy Focaccia Recipe & Tutorial | Mindful Baking | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
Hi everybody! Click below for the full ingredients and description...

In todays Mindful Baking video we are diving head first into the world of bread, but don't let that scare you! This Focaccia recipe could not be easier and it is the prefect example of how mindful baking can be. The processes involved in bread making are simple, it just requires a lot of time and attention. Whilst the dough is rising and proving you can get on with your daily tasks but when its time to knead or work the dough you can really focus on the job in hand for the 15/20 minutes required. 

Sam & I love to hear about what some of your favourite go-to recipes are and the bakes that take you back to the beginning of your baking journey are. Let us know in the comments box and if you like this video please give it a thumbs up. 

Sally x 

-----------
For the starter: 
1.5 tsp active dried yeast 
15g sugar or honey 
300g 00, bread, or plain white flour
420ml tepid water 

For the dough:
320g 00, bread, or plain white flour
30ml good quality olive oil 
15g sea salt

For the topping: 
Good quality olive oil 
Fresh rosemary 
Flaked sea salt

Preheat oven to 220C fan assisted 
Bake for 10 minutes then reduce the temperature to 190C and bake for a further 20 minutes or until the focaccia is golden brown and crisp on top.

videos.title: Swedish Chokladbollar Chocolate Balls | Mindful Baking
videos.description:
Hi everybody! Click below for the full ingredients and description...

Today we are exploring the Swedish concept of Fika which loosely translates to 'coffee break'. And although coffee is involved, Fika, means so much more. It is in fact a ritual and perhaps even a state of mind that the Swedes celebrate on a daily basis. It is time out, away from work duties, to socialise and allow our brains and bodies to refresh. This is such a beautiful concept and resonates with the idea behind these mindful baking videos that Sam and I are creating. 

So what better treat to make than Swedish Chokladbollar. These simple no bake bites are quick, easy and delicious. We hope that you will bake along and perhaps share your treats with colleagues by suggesting a short Fika in your work day.

Sally x 

We would love to know what some of your favourite go-to recipes are and the bakes that take you back to the beginning of your baking journey. Let us know in the comments box and if you like this video please give it a thumbs up. 

12 Chokladbollar: 
115g softened butter 
100g caster or granulated sugar
100g oats 
25g cocoa powder 
Pinch of salt 
1tsp vanilla extract 
2 tbsp cooled coffee (optional)

Mindful Bakes ep3:  Plant-Based Chocolate Courgette Cake Recipe
videos.description:
Hi everybody! Click below for the full ingredients and description...

It can be hard to find moments of peace and quiet in a fast-paced world. In creating the Mindful Baking videos we wanted to highlight how important, how enjoyable and how delicious it is to slow down, get yourself into the kitchen and take some time to bake. I find that connecting with the ingredients and processes involved in baking can be really good for de-stressing, and I'd like to share some of those moments with you through these videos. Sam and I want to cover simple, honest recipes that are soothing and enjoyable to prepare, without to need for too much equipment, and we'd love it if you bake along, or just watch along.

This time we're baking a completely plant-based, decadent Chocolate Courgette Cake.

Sally x 

We would love to know what some of your favourite go-to recipes are and the bakes that take you back to the beginning of your baking journey. Let us know in the comments box and if you like this video please give it a thumbs up. 

Tin size: 2 x 8" Sandwich pans
However you can use any tin, even a loaf tin would work great.

Cake Ingredients: 
200ml Plant-based milk (I used almond milk)
100ml Vegetable or sunflower oil 
320g Caster or granulated sugar 
200g Plain flour 
70g Cocoa powder 
1/2 tsp Sea Salt 
1 tsp Bicarbonate of soda 
1.5 tsp Baking powder
1 Courgette (approx 180g)

For the ganache: 
200g Dark chocolate 
100g Plant-based butter 
 
-----------

Ever since we put this cookie in our London Soho Bakery, you guys have been salivating for it. It's finally here! A dark double chocolate chip cookie, laced with Oreo crumble, white chocolate chips, 54% chocolate chips, filled with a molten creamy white chocolate filling. We love these cookies because they're thick, ooey, gooey and that perfect balance between a crisp outer and a dense, soft, almost underbaked centre. 

Our Cookie & Cream Cookies will be in store all this week, so come by and grab one, or a few! 

C&D Soho:
1 Kingly Court, 
Carnaby 
W1B 5PW

INGREDIENTS:

12 tbsp White Chocolate Spread
230g Cold, Chopped Butter
160g Light Brown Sugar
160g Caster Sugar
300g White Chocolate Chips
100g Dark Chocolate Chips 
50g Crushed Oreo Crumbs 
300g Plain Flour
130g Self Raising Flour 
70g Black Cocoa Powder 
1 tsp Sea Salt
2 tsp Baking Powder
2 Eggs
1 Egg Yolk
50g Crushed Oreo Cookies for Rolling
Makes 12 Cookies

videos.title: Mindful Baking ep2: Marmalade Loaf Cake Recipe
videos.description:
Hi everybody! Click below for the full ingredients and description...
Sally x

It can be hard to find moments of peace and quiet in a fast-paced world. In creating the Mindful Baking videos we wanted to highlight how important, how enjoyable and how delicious it is to slow down, get yourself into the kitchen and take some time to bake. I find that connecting with the ingredients and processes involved in baking can be really good for de-stressing, and I'd like to share some of those moments with you through these videos. Sam and I want to cover simple, honest recipes that are soothing and enjoyable to prepare, without to need for too much equipment, and we'd love it if you bake along, or just watch along.

This time we're baking a delicious, super-moist Marmalade Loaf Cake.


Sally x 

We would love to know what some of your favourite go-to recipes are and the bakes that take you back to the beginning of your baking journey. Let us know in the comments box and if you like this video please give it a thumbs up. 

Loaf tin size: 25 x 12 cm 
However you can use any tin, even a round cake tin 8-9”

INGREDIENTS:
240ml Orange Juice
180g Sunflower or flavourless oil
4 Large Free Range Eggs
160g Marmalade
Zest of 1 Orange
100g Caster Sugar
180g Semolina
70g Desiccated Coconut
90g Plain Flour
2tsp Baking Powder
20g Ground Almonds

FOR THE GLAZE:
150g Caster Sugar
100ml Water
Juice 1/2 Orange

It can be hard to find moments of peace and quiet in a fast-paced world. In creating the Mindful Baking videos we wanted to highlight how important, how enjoyable and how delicious it is to slow down, get yourself into the kitchen and take some time to bake. I find that connecting with the ingredients and processes involved in baking can be really good for de-stressing, and I'd like to share some of those moments with you through these videos. December London Bakery Tour London - the best bakeries and bakes in London! | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
IT’S CHRISTMASSSSSS!!! You guys went H.A.M. on the last bakery tour that we did when it was still scorching hot. We thought we’d bring you a Festive Winter edition, and gallivant around London to see what some of our favourite bakeries have to offer at this time of year. 

We started at Fortitude Bakery, famous for it’s viennoiserie and beignets, then headed over to Ô Craquelin Pâtisserie for delectable petit gateaux. Stopped by the epitome of standing inside Christmas, Bageriet (A Swedish bakery), checked out Jemma’s favourite, Arôme Bakery, St. John’s Bakery, Chin Chin Dessert Club and finally back on home soil at Crumbs and Doilies. 

Click on the link to the map below to follow along the tour:

videos.description:
HAPPY HALLOWEEN!! 🎃👻🦇

You may know this already but we LOVE Halloween over here at Crumbs & Doilies so it only seemed fitting to share with you lovely lot the newest cake that's hitting our website, the Chocolate & Vanilla Halloween cake! It features our brand new, super soft vanilla cake sandwiched and iced with the most delicious Chocolate Cream Cheese buttercream and finished with the cutest little buttercream ghosts 👻 - YOU'RE GONNA LOVE IT!

videos.description:
IT'S ICE CREAM SEASON 🍦🧡 and not just any ice cream season - Salted Caramel Toffee Fudge season - hell yeah 😎. Silky salted caramel ice cream rippled with a caramel sauce and studded with fudge pieces - this is the dreamiest ice cream you'll make this year. Eat it in a waffle cone or in a bowl with extra sauce and you've got yourselves an absolute TREAT! 

We made this using an ice cream machine, you can also make it without one, Jemma explains all in the video!

videos.description:
We are O B S E S S E D with the Milk Choc & Passion Fruit flavour combo, it's been a favourite of Dane's for a while now and we thought you'd like to see one of our fave cupcakes brought to life!
Super squishy passionfruit & yogurt sponge filled with a smooth passion fruit curd, topped with the silkiest milk chocolate swiss meringue buttercream and finished with a dollop of curd & the tangiest milk choc & passionfruit disc! Get ready, this recipe makes 12 cupcakes!

1 ½ tbsp freeze-dried passion fruit chunks 

MILK CHOC SWISS MERINGUE BUTTERCREAM

¼ tsp sea salt300g caster sugar 
½ tsp vanilla extract 
360g unsalted butter
300g milk chocolate

videos.description:
GUYS! Is this the best brownie we've ever made? I actually think it might be 😏, fudgey brownie stuffed with mini creme eggs, topped with a duo of eggy looking layers that are melt in the mouth delicious and finished with chocolate ganache and even more creme eggs? Absolute filth and absolutely DREAMY! you'll find everything you'll need below :)

videos.description:
We're into the second week of the Bake the Book Challenge and we couldn't;t be more excited to share the recipe for the Breakfast Bowl Cupcakes!! It's a super moist yogurt sponge, filled to the brim with a blueberry goo, topped with a duo of yogurt & blueberry buttercreams and finished with a selection of homemade granola & fruit! Simply divine and potentially your new favourite breakfast 😏...  Don't forget to use the hashtag #crumbsanddoiliesbook when posting snaps of your bakes because we love seeing how you get on!!

30g honey
10g light brown sugar

¼ tsp sea salt
450g icing sugar

1–2 tbsp plain yoghurt 

ADDITIONAL TOPPINGS (OPTIONAL)banana chips, freeze dried raspberry or strawberry pieces, coconut flakes etc.

videos.description:
Do you have masses of panettone knocking about after Christmas? Oh you do! WELL,  this is the recipe for you! All you'll need is a few ingredients, a waffle machine (or a frying pan) and your favourite toppings, because Jemma's showing you how to make these DELISH Panettone Bread & Butter Waffles!! They're the perfect combo of all your favourite desserts all rolled into one seriously tasty treat! Don't forget to tag us in your snaps over on Instagram because we love seeing how you get on recreating these bakes!

250g panettone
2 eggs
40g caster sugar
120g milk
120g cream
Pinch of salt
1/2 tsp vanilla
25g butter
30g choc chips

This makes 4-5 large waffles.

videos.description:
If you've ever wondered 'what's the best apple crumble recipe out there..?' THE ANSWER IS THIS ONE! Sticky, spiced apples topped with a buttery crumble mixed with crunchy streusel and finished with a glug of hot brown sugar custard.. errrrmgad😍 IT'S SO GOOD!! Make it. Love it. Thank us later! 

Most importantly HAPPY NEW YEAR!!!! Thank you so much for all your support throughout the year, we appreciate you all and couldn't be more grateful! Here's to a wonderful 2023 🥳

10x7" Rectangle baking tin (or something similar).

STREUSEL
45g Plain Flour
30g Demerera Sugar
30g Melted Butter
¼ tsp Cinnamon

CRUMBLE
120g Cold Butter
180g Plain Flour
40g Caster Sugar
25g Demerera Sugar
Pinch of Salt

APPLES

2-3 tbsp Caster Sugar
¾ tsp Cinnamon
Pinch of Cloves 

BROWN SUGAR CUSTARD
3 Egg Yolks
90g Light Brown Sugar 
30g Cornflour
Pinch of Salt
500ml Whole Milk
200ml Double Cream
1 tsp Vanilla

Dane is back this week with one of our favourite cupcakes that you'll regularly find in the Crumbs & Doilies shop.. The Bourbon Biscuit Cupcake! Now, if you're not familiar with them, bourbon biscuits are a crunchy chocolate biscuit filled with chocolate cream (no alcohol involved!) and they're absolutely delicious - a true biscuit tin staple! So we OBVIOUSLY had to cupcakeify them! 

CUPCAKES

1/8 tsp Bicarbonate of Soda

300g Caster Sugar
300g 70% Chocolate, Melted
360g Soft Butter

FILLING
Ganache:
70g 54% Chocolate
70g Double Cream

65g Double Cream
Bourbon biscuits to finish

videos.description:
Jemma is back with the recipe of D R E A M S this autumn! These pumpkin spice s'mores cookies are going to knock your socks off! The softest, chewiest pumpkin spice cookie, filled to the brim with toasted Italian meringue and & melted dark chocolate - THEY'RE INSANE!!

½ grated nutmeg
Pinch of ground cloves

ITALIAN MERINGUE

20g glucose
75g water
60g egg white
Pinch salt
½ tsp vanilla

FILLING
100-150g melted dark chocolate

Anywhere that has unlimited slushies filled with rum and topped with a tiny umbrella AND a dedicated karaoke room is my kinda party!

The cake consisted of:

TOP TIER - Pistachio & White Choc 

It's truly a masterpiece, so grab a coffee or a tea, sit back and enjoy! 🥰

Pina Colada Coconut, Pineapple & Rum Layer Cake | Recipe and Decoration tutorial | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
The cake of the summer? I think so!! Or as Dane refers to it "a celebration of summer" 🥥🍍. This cake really is all of that and a little more - 4 layers of coconut sponge, drenched in a pineapple syrup, filled to the brim with rum swiss meringue buttercream & pineapple jam, finished with toasted coconut and pineapple flowers - whats not to love?! Don't forget to tag us in your snaps over on instagram and use the hashtag #cupcakejemma so we can see them! 

Sponge:
330g Butter
135g Vegetable Oil
500g Caster Sugar
8 Eggs
100g Desiccated Coconut
400g Self-Raising Flour
6 tbsp Coconut Cream
1/2 tsp Vanilla
Pinch of Salt

Pineapple Jam:
600g Pineapple Chunks (Approx 3/4 Large Pineapple)
250g Water
420g Caster Sugar
1 Lime - Juice only

Pineapple Flowers:
1/4 Fresh Pineapple (use the same one as for the jam)

Rum Swiss Meringue Buttercream:
450g Caster Sugar
6 Egg Whites
600g Soft Unsalted Butter
1/4 tsp Salt
8-10 tbsp White Rum

To Decorate:
180g Desiccated Coconut 

Cake truffle tutorial https://youtu.be/GACgPbU_ciw
Grab the best vanilla just here https://cupcakejemma.com/collections/cake-tools/products/vanilla-extract-50ml

Neapolitan Bundt Cake Recipe | Strawberry, Vanilla and Chocolate sponge | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
You guys should know by now that we absolutely LOVE a bundt cake and also ice cream... So it only seemed fitting to put the two together and make a Neapolitan bundt cake right?! Jemma's showing you how to make this super easy recipe - 3 flavours all in one? Some would call this a triple threat... Super squishy & moist vanilla, chocolate & strawberry pound cake all marbled together and topped off with a crackly glaze? PERFECTION!

290g Soft Butter
185g Cream Cheese
415g Caster Sugar
5 Eggs
Pinch of Salt
1/2 tsp Vanilla extract

Vanilla:
100g plain flour
1 tsp vanilla
1/4 tsp Baking Powder

Strawberry:
80g plain flour
3 tbsp strawberry powder (15g)
Food colouring
1/4 tsp Baking Powder

Chocolate:
30g cocoa
70g plain flour
1/2 tsp vanilla
1/4 tsp Baking Powder

Glaze:
450g Icing sugar
9tbsp Milk

We used the Heritage Bundt tin which was very kindly gifted from Nordic Ware.

Perfect Key Lime Pie Recipe | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
Looking for a dessert to wow everyone (and I do mean everyone!) then look no further! The perfect summer pie consists of a zesty, smooth lime filling, a spicy biscuit crust, topped off with the silkiest Chantilly cream, and lucky for you guys, Dane's got you covered with this DELISH Key Lime Pie!

350g Ginger Nuts Biscuits
95g Butter

FILLING
1 can condensed milk (397g)
5 Egg Yolks
150ml Lime Juice + 4 zest
200g Double Cream

TOPPING
280g Double Cream
20g Icing Sugar
1/4 tsp Vanilla

videos.title: Chocolate Dipped Jaffa Cake Cupcakes Recipe | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
If you've joined our Bake Club, you will have seen Dane testing these Jaffa cake cupcakes last week, so we OBVS had to share the recipe! Sally's showing you just how it's done and we think you'll agree, they look INSANE! Light vanilla sponge filled with delish orange marmalade, topped with a decadent orange ganache, a thick slab of orange jelly, all dunked in dark chocolate - what's not to love!?

You can grab the nozzles, piping bags and cupcake cases Sally used in the video at the link below 
https://cupcakejemma.com/collections/shop-everything 

CUPCAKES
125g Butter
125g caster sugar
2 eggs
125g SR flour
1/4 tsp bicarbonate
1/2 tsp vanilla
1.5 tbsp Whole Milk 
150g marmalade

GANACHE
300g 54 % choc
110g cream
2 orange zest
40g orange juice
150g butter

JELLY
200g jelly cubes
430g boiling water 

DECORATION
200g 54% choc
5g cocoa butter

videos.description:
Jemma's current favourite thing? ROASTING STRAWBERRIES! You wouldn't believe how much better they taste, pair them with fresh mint and you'll be in a summery dream! Sally and Dane are joining Jemma in the kitchen this time to bring these flavours to life! 

Don't forget to tag us over on Instagram and use the hashtag #cupcakejemma so we can see how you get on!

ROASTED STRAWBERRIES

ITALIAN MERINGUE

videos.description:
There are 2 birthdays in the Cupcake Jemma team this week - Sally & Sam are both celebrating so OBVIOUSLY a cake was needed, and sometimes, a single layer of deliciously moist vanilla funfetti cake, topped with silky swiss meringue buttercream does the trick!

Enjoy!

SWISS MERINGUE BUTTERCREAM

videos.description:

For the Ganache:

30g Cream

videos.title: Lemon & Amaretti Jubilee Party Cake with wildflower decoration | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
I'm not sure if you're aware, but there's a Platinum Jubilee happening this weekend 👀?! Now there will be street parties up and down the country, and what's something everyone needs to bring to a party? CAKE! Specifically this one! We've pulled flavours from the winning Jubilee Pudding Competition, and come up with our take on the classic Victoria Sponge - introducing the lemon and amaretti cake! 

We want to know what you're up to this weekend so let us know in the comments below the vid! And share your photos with us on Instagram by using #cupcakejemma 
-------------------
Lemon Curd
65g caster sugar
65g lemon juice
zest of 1 lemon
1 egg
3 egg yolks
50g cold chopped butter

Sponge
Preheat oven to 170C fan assisted (190C non fan)
250g soft butter
250g caster sugar
zest of 1 lemon
4 eggs
250g self raising flour
pinch of salt
2 tbsp lemon
½ tsp vanilla extract

Amaretto Cream
300g double cream
4 tbsp Amaretto liqueur
1 tbsp icing sugar, sifted

To finish:
sprinkling of caster sugar
edible flowers
AMARETTI BISCUITS!!!

-----------------------------

videos.title: Ultimate Gooey Brownie with Caramelised Chocolate and Macadamias. GLUTEN FREE | Cupcake Jemma
videos.description:
This is Sally's all time favourite brownie and it just happens to be Gluten Free. It's made using ground Almonds instead of flour which gives the Brownie the most amazingly fudgey gooey texture. You can add any chocolate chips or nuts to this brownie but we have gone for our faves - Caramelised White Chocolate (duh) and Macadamia Nuts which have a beautiful buttery flavour and texture. Our top tip with any brownie but specifically this one, is to chill it in the fridge overnight (or for a few hours) to help achieve the dream texture. 

We can't wait to see how you get on so make sure to tag your Instagram posts with #cupcakejemma so we can see and share and marvel!

See below for full list of ingredients. If you want to get your hands on a printable/downloadable recipe complete with ingredients, instructions and photos then join our Bake Club where you can get one of these EVERY WEEK!

videos.description:
Nanaimo Bars??? We'd never heard of them either. That was until Dane whipped us up a batch that we demolished in seconds and then begged him to make us more! These Nanaimo Bars have a crunchy and decadent Chocolate, Coconut and Walnut base, a super creamy Custard Buttercream filling and a snappy Chocolate topping. They really do have every texture going on to make them the ultimate treat. PLUS they are so quick and easy to make which makes them a perfect bake to make with kids! 

Why not play around with different flavour combinations too and let us know how you get on over on Instagram but tagging your photos with #cupcakejemma 

We cannot wait to be baking in the brandy new Cupcake Jemma studio. For behind the scenes sneaky peaks join our Bake Club! http://patreon.com/cupcakejemma.com

videos.title: Homemade Tea Cakes Recipe! Crunchy Biscuit, Chewy Marshmallow Chocolate | Cupcake Jemma Channel
videos.description:
Tea time treats don't get better than this. These Tea Cakes may look small but they sure are mighty! They have every texture and flavour needed to create the perfect treat; Melt in the mouth buttery Shortbread biscuits, topped with bouncy light Marshmallow enrobed in perfectly tempered snappy dark Chocolate. YUM! 

If you have a silicone mould you will be able to get a beautifully smooth finish on your Tea Cakes but don't worry it's not essential, you can pipe your Mallow directly on top on the Shortbread and they drizzle or dunk them in the melted Chocolate. 

See below for the recipe or join our Bake Club to get a full downloadable and printable pdf of the recipe with instructions and photos. 
http://patreon.com/cupcakejemma.com

------------------------------------------------

75g Softened unsalted Butter 
30g Caster Sugar 
115g Plan Flour 
1/2 tsp Salt 
1/2 tsp Vanilla Extract 

For the Marshmallow 
12g Powdered Gelatine 
60ml + 30ml Water 
90g + 90g Glucose Syrup 
75g Caster Sugar
1 tsp Vanilla Extract 

For the Chocolate 
500g good quality Chocolate

videos.title: Show-Stopping Desserts for an Unforgettable Bridal Shower | Jacques Pépin Celebrates
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin and daughter Claudine prepare dessert recipes for a bridal shower. When Claudine is asked to make desserts for a fancy bridal shower, Jacques has one word for her: meringue. He prepares elegant Almond Floating Islands in a pool of Rum Custard Sauce and buttery caramel. Then it's on to an Orange Vacherin Jeannette—layers of meringue with a frozen orange-cream filling—that's as easy as it is spectacular. Volcano Surprise with Lemon Mousseline Cake is Jacques' whimsical take on Baked Alaska—a little lighter, and a lot more dramatic. And his foolproof Mocha Success Cake is made with a rich buttercream, sandwiched between layers of crunchy hazelnut meringue.

Recipes in this video:
00:00 Mocha success cake recipe
8:35 Almond floating islands with custard sauce
10:54 Orange vacherian Jeanette cake with orange cream filling
16:18 Volcano surprise with lemon mousseline cake recipe

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Summer Recipes | Celebrates
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin and daughter Claudine prepare summer recipes for a French-American mash-up celebration of the 4th of July and Bastille Day. A whole, poached Salmon with a light, creamy Mousseline Sauce is an easy, elegant way to feed a large group. It's followed by charming little crocks filled with Rillettes of Rabbit—the classic French party spread that's perfect with country bread and a crisp white wine. For the main course: a whole Grilled Shoulder of Veal, served with lemon-herb butter. And Jacques' creamy Potato-and-Corn Packages are just right for the occasion. For dessert, there's an all-American sponge cake soaked in sweet summer berries and brandy with a silky custard sauce and a very French Chocolate Gourmand—a decadent chocolate mousse you can serve right out of the bowl.

Recipes in this video:

videos.title: Jacques Pépin Schools Us With His Graduation Party Menu | Celebrates
videos.description:
A family friend is graduating from college, and Jacques Pépin and daughter Claudine need no better excuse to create a splendid al fresco buffet lunch. There's delicate smoked trout with a creamy egg-and-pepper salad—just right for a sunny June afternoon. And Jacques' classic chicken galantine—a boneless chicken stuffed with pork, black mushrooms, pistachios and armagnac, poached in a rich stock and served in it's own aspic—might be the world's most elegant picnic food. A succulent baked ham Georgia with a tangy apricot-mustard glaze and a deep, sweet-savory peach sauce is an impressive do-ahead entrée for a party. Jacques pairs it with a simple side dish of buttered red Swiss chard. For the cheese course, he shows Claudine how to make creamy fresh-herb fromage blanc served with roasted garlic and garnished with a beautiful coral tree. Two show-stopping desserts complete the buffet. Chocolate cloud cake is a spectacular layered confection, with the contrasting textures of fluffy sponge cake, cognac whipped cream, and crunchy chocolate cookie crust, all topped with a bittersweet chocolate glaze. And cherry-raspberry pillow tops off the summer fruit desserts—fresh cherries and berries baked in a free-from puff-pastry pie

Recipes in this video:

16:02 Fromage Blanc Jean-Victor with roasted garlic and coral tree

27:03 Take a tour of Jacques Pepin's herb garden

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Guide to a Fantastic Easter Dinner | Celebrates
videos.description:
In this surprise-filled celebration of Spring, Jacques Pépin and daughter Claudine prepare a sumptuous Easter dinner for friends. Salmon steaks with a tangy, fresh sorrel sauce and delicate little cucumber balls make an easy first course. And of course, there's lamb—in this case, spring lamb loins in ambush, coated with a ground lamb and vegetable mousse and served over fresh fava beans. Jacques' leek-and-mushroom pie brings just the right earthy flavors to go with the lamb. And on the side, there are roasted eggplant "cushions" garnished with lightly stewed tomatoes; and artichoke bottoms stuffed with brown-butter spinach, topped with a golden-brown gratin sauce. The meal ends as it began, with more grown-up Easter eggs: fluffy caramel snow eggs floating in custard cream. In a classic Pépin pairing, this ethereal dessert is served with Jacques down-to-earth, old-fashioned oatmeal cookies dipped in sumptuous chocolate.  

Recipes in this video:

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Classic Christmas Recipes
videos.description:
These Jacques Pépin Christmas recipes may spark new traditions. Whether it's the Buche de Noel yule log with mint leaves and chocolate bark or the succulent braised duck with glazed shallots and sweet potatoes, there is a recipe here to suit everyone at your table. Jacques shares wonderful techniques for how to make stock and demi-glace, how to shave truffles, and more. Jacques' wife Gloria and daughter Claudine lend a hand in the kitchen making this a sweet family special.

Recipes included in this video:
English Christmas pudding with hard sauce, Buche de Noel, foie gras with port wine aspic, salt-cured foie gras with pistachio, braised duck with glazed shallots and sweet potatoes, stock, demi-glace, black truffle salad, home-smoked salmon and cucumber salad, oysters on the half shell with mignonette sauce

videos.title: Classic Thanksgiving Recipes from Jacques Pépin & Family 🦃🍂
videos.description:
There are some years where you just feel like phoning it in for the holidays. This is not that video. This is Jacques Pepin at his most skilled and thoughtful, preparing elaborate Thanksgiving dinner recipes with his family. This episode opens with Jacques' late wife Gloria helping to prepare the salmon appetizer while daughter Claudine assists with the turkey dinner and sides. Many friends drop in along the way and lend their expertise on everything from mushroom foraging to apple cider fermentation. True to form, Jacques peppers in pieces of wisdom throughout the video: why to cook the stuffing separately, why you should steam the turkey before roasting, how to bone fish, and more. The holidays can be stressful and filled with feelings, let the soothing voice of Jacques Pépin guide you through it.

Included in this video: Salmon appetizers (salmon tartare, herb-crusted cured salmon), chocolate bourbon prune cake, roast turkey with mushroom stuffing, butternut squash gratin, brussels sprouts, and more.

videos.title: 3 Amazing Mussels Recipes from Jacques Pépin | Cooking at Home
videos.description:
This is a fantastic episode packed with great tips. Jacques Pépin demonstrates 3 ways to prepare mussels. He starts with Billi Bi soup, a creamy, flavorful soup with a mussel base. The second is Moules Provencale, broiled with butter, garlic, parsley, and bread crumbs, it's a smilar preparation to escargot (minus the snails!). The last recipe is Mussels Remoulade, a rich, slightly salty mixture, served with large lettuce head leaves. If you try one of these recipes, let us know in the comments!

What you'll need:
2 lbs mussels, 2 cloves garlic, 1 cup wine, 1/2 cup chopped onion, salt and pepper, olive oil
Billi Bi Soup: 1 1/2 tsp potato starch, 1 tbsp water, dash of Sriracha, 1/2 cup cream, chives
Moules Provencale: 2 tbsp butter, 1 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp parsley, tsp garlic, salt, pepper, 1/2 slice bread (similar to escargot)
Remoulade: 1/3 cup mayo, 1 tsp Dijon, 1 tsp capers, dash of Tabasco, gherkin or cornichon, 1 tsp anchovy paste, 1 tbsp parsley, large lettuce leaves,

Jacques Pépin wraps up the episode with the tastiest tomato sandwich using crunchy bread and anchovies. With grocery prices the way they are, these adopting these tricks are more useful than ever.

Leftover bread, tomato, salt, olive oil, 2 anchovy filets, butter

videos.description:

videos.title: Try These Sausage Lettuce Wraps | Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin's grilled merguez sausage lettuce wraps are a great grab-and-go option for your next backyard summer party, especially for those who are trying to shun the bun and cut back on carbs.

Though the cilantro and mint in this recipe are reminiscent of Vietnamese rolls, merguez sausage is most often found in North Africa. Lamb is traditionally used in merguez and that's what Jacques uses in his version here.

What you'll need:
1 12oz lamb shoulder chop, 1 lb hot Italian sausage, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp chili powder, salt, romaine leaves, pickled onions, cilantro, mint

Jacques Pépin Cooking At Home: Grilled Merguez with Lettuce, Cilantro, and Mint

videos.description:
Let's all welcome the return of one of the most versatile fruits by making Jacques Pépin's baked stuffed tomato recipe. This juicy plant-based dish is satisfying as a main course. If you're not a tomato fan, you could also use the stuffing recipe to top baked fish or chicken. Jacques uses what he has on hand so feel free to improvise.

2 slices of bread, 1 large egg, chives, 1/2 cup spinach, 1/4 cup Swiss cheese, 1/4 cup onions, 2 - 3 mushrooms,  1 clove garlic, 2 large tomatoes, salt and pepper, olive oil, parmesan cheese. Optional: sausage.

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Moist and Crispy Garlic Chicken Recipe | Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin's garlic chicken recipe is all about the technique. Jacques recommends using chicken thigh and browning it skin side-down in the pan to produce a crispy top with moist underside. He deglazes the chicken in vinegar and uses the leftover chicken grease to flash fry the garlic. This dish is also known as poulet au vinaigre.

What you'll need:
2 bone-in chicken thighs, salt and pepper, 3 garlic cloves, 3 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp water, 2 tbsp ketchup, dash of Tabasco, 1/2 cup olives (optional), chives or parsley

Jacques Pépin Cooking At Home: Grilled Chicken with Garlic and Vinegar

videos.description:

4 oz. salmon belly, 1/4 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp sugar, 2 tsp toasted sesame oil, 2 tsp oyster sauce, 
1 tsp sriracha, 2 tbsp chives, 2 tsp sesame seeds

videos.description:

1 can (lrg) sardines in tomato sauce,  lettuce, 1/2 sweet onion, 1/2 tomato, dash red wine vinegar, black pepper, fresh herbs (for garnish)

videos.title: Try These Spicy Chicken Feet from Jacques Pépin 🐔 | Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Chicken feet are a delicacy in many parts of the world. Though not as familiar to many in the West, Jacques Pépin's chicken feet recipe uses a spicy Chinese chili garlic sauce that gives this dish a rush of flavor. This recipe would traditionally be thickened with arrowroot or corn starch, but Jacques substitutes ketchup. 

Are you going to sample chicken feet tonight? Let us know how they turn out!

1 lb. chicken feet (trimmed), 1 tbsp oil, 2 scallions, 1 tbsp grated garlic, 1 tbsp grated ginger, salt, 1/4 cup chicken stock, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp (hot) chili garlic sauce, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 3 tbsp ketchup, cilantro

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Famous Clam Chowder Recipe 🥣  | Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Is anything more warm and comforting than a bowl of Jacques Pépin's famous New England clam chowder? This classic recipe uses a medley of bacon, baby clams, leek, potatoes, corn, and, of course, heavy cream. At one point in his career, Jacques would make a version of this that would feed hundreds of people while working at Howard Johnson's, but for this recipe, you can comfortably feed a family of 4 - 6 😋.

What you'll need:
2 slices bacon, olive oil, 1leek, 1 clove garlic, fresh thyme, jalapeno, 2 cups clam juice, 1 cup water, 1/2 lb potatoes, salt, pepper, 2 tbsp flour, 1 ear corn, 2 10oz cans of baby clams, 1/2 cup heavy cream, chives.

videos.title: A History of the Fillmore Neighborhood in San Francisco | KQED
videos.description:
If you know anything about this neighborhood, it's probably because it houses an auditorium of the same name, where the Jefferson Airplane and the Grateful Dead helped shape American music in the 1960s. This documentary, produced in 1999 as part of KQED's series on San Francisco neighborhoods, goes even deeper to tell a dramatic story: the rise and fall  -- and rise again -- of San Francisco's premiere Black community.

The program features a remarkable group of on-air participants and storytellers, from mayors and musicians to journalists and community activists, who experienced the Fillmore as both the best and worst of 20th century life. The documentary draws on a rich archive of photographs, film, and music of the neighborhood, helped in part by home movies and photographs from KQED viewers.

Narrated by Ossie Davis.

videos.title: Jacques Pépin on Julia Child: The Key to their 50 Year Friendship | KQED Ask Jacques

For us, it will always be Jacques Pépin and Julia Child. Jacques and Julia brought joy and accessibility to the kitchen and to television audiences for decades. In this micro interview, Jacques shares a behind-the-scenes memory from working with Julia and celebrates a friendship that spanned half a century.

videos.title: These Incredible Arches Once Lit Up San Francisco's Fillmore Neighborhood | KQED
videos.description:
After surviving the 1906 earthquake, San Francisco’s Fillmore neighborhood became an increasingly bustling and diverse shopping district. The Fillmore Arches were beautiful decorative ironwork pieces outfitted with hundreds of dazzling lights that criss-crossed over the busy intersections, beginning with California at McAllister. These displays helped earn the Fillmore a new moniker as “The Most Highly Illuminated Thoroughfare in America”. 

Although the arches are no longer around today, their memory is a testament to the ever-changing landscape and communities of San Francisco. 

Learn more about the Fillmore neighborhood on PBS Video: https://www.pbs.org/video/the-hidden-cities-of-san-francisco-the-fillmore-6sltwo/

These clips were pulled from the KQED archives.

videos.description:

videos.title: Best Pork and Red Beans Recipe - Gloria's Favorite! | Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home  | KQED

Jacques Pépin shares his recipe for pork and red beans with rice. This recipe is special, not only because it looks SO delicious, but it was also one of his late wife Gloria's favorite dishes. Jacques suggest cooking for 1 hour in the pressure cooker, but make sure to test along the way as cooking times may vary. Dinner guests will be fighting for an invite when this Puerto Rican/Cuban classic in on the menu. Happy cooking!

What you'll need:

videos.title: Armistead Maupin on Acceptance | KQED #Shorts
videos.description: Before “Tales of the City” became a beloved series of novels and a hit video miniseries, acclaimed author Armistead Maupin was writing his popular column for the San Francisco Chronicle in the late 1970s. Former KQED reporter Randy Shilts talked with him back then about what it was like to be out at work –– and his response was priceless.

videos.title: How to Make Melba Toast | Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin shares a history lesson and a recipe for melba toast. You may have heard of melba toast or its dessert recipe cousin peach melba, but what is it? And where did it come from? Jacques shines some light on the French chef and the Australian Soprano singer who inspired this dish. Melba toast could be a good compromise for those who are trying to watch their carbs but not ready to give up bread entirely yet.

What you'll need:
Bread. Maybe some butter. That's it!

videos.title: Legendary Actor Divine Explains Why Drag Isn’t a Drag | KQED #shorts
videos.description:
"I think with all of the horrible things that are going down in the world ... People need to laugh. People need to have a good time to let loose" - Divine, 1970.

Legislation that’s under consideration throughout the country in 2023 could impact the rights of the LGBTQIA+ community — including laws that ban or censor drag shows. 

Here’s what KQED correspondent Josh Decologon has to say about the current state of affairs. We also dip into the KQED archives for a quote from legendary drag performer Divine during a 1970 performance at San Francisco’s The Neon Woman club. 

#KQED #Pride #LGBTQ #KQEDThrowback

videos.title: How Did Pride Get Started? | KQED #Shorts
videos.description:
June is Pride Month! Ever wonder how Pride first got started? We’re taking a look back at the historic 7th Annual Gay Freedom Day Parade that filled the streets of San Francisco in 1978 –– and where the iconic rainbow Pride flag made its debut. #Pride #LGBTQ #Shorts #KQEDThrowback

videos.title: Dandelion Salad with Pancetta is a Summertime Delight | Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
As spring turns into summer, nothing tastes so seasonally appropriate as Jacques Pépin's recipe for dandelion salad with pancetta. Jacques tips for how to select and trim your dandelion greens will make wild foraging a breeze, even if it's your first time. But if you're not keen on digging in the yard, many chain grocery stores stock dandelion greens already trimmed and prepped. Pancetta (or bacon) with garlic and anchovy paste forms the base of the dressing and Jacques recommends a 7-minute boiled egg to accompany this salad, so the yolk is soft and bright. We think this might be a new favorite.

What you'll need:
3 cups dandelion greens, 3 slices bacon or pancetta, eggs, olive oil, 2 cloves garlic, 2 tsps anchovy paste, salt and pepper, dash of vinegar, croutons, 1 soft boiled egg.

Jacques Pépin Cooking At Home: Dandelion Salad

videos.title: Classic Roast Chicken Ultimate Guide | Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin shares a gloriously simple but detailed recipe for classic roast chicken. He includes techniques for how to remove the wishbone like a pro, how to truss a chicken with twine to keep its shape, how to make sure a chicken is evenly roasted, and a great way to baste it using only its own juices, butter, potato starch, and chicken stock.

Once the chicken is roasted, Jacques teaches us how to carve and present the dish with a side of fresh watercress. Elegant and delicious, this roast chicken recipe is a classic for a reason.

What you'll need:
3 1/2 lb Chicken, 1 tbsp butter, salt and pepper, 1/2 cup chicken stock, dash of soy sauce (for color), 1 tsp potato starch, watercress

Jacques Pépin Cooking At Home: Classic Roast Chicken

videos.description:
Jacques Pépin shares his recipe for a hearty daube du beef country stew inspired by the French city of Arles, where Van Gogh made many of his most beloved paintings. The thickening agent for this stew is made with hazelnuts, bread, parsely, and garlic. Jacques uses garden-fresh vegetables including zucchini, squash, tomato, and plenty of garlic in a visually appealing side dish of zucchini fans. For dessert, Jacques prepares a goblet of fruit, black currant syrup, creme de cassis, and Swiss wine.

videos.description:

videos.title: Cozy Black Bean Soup Recipe | Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin shares a simple and delicious black bean soup recipe. This recipe freezes well so it's a great meal prep option. Jacques suggests blending a portion to give it a creamy consistency and shares some garnish options, including cilantro, hardboiled egg, and even his wife's favorite - sliced bananas.

What you'll need:
1 lb dry black beans, 2 qt. water, 1 qt. chicken stock, 1/2 cup rice, 1/2 tsp cumin 1/2 chili pepper, salt. cilantro, 2 cups salsa, 6 cloves garlic, 1 leek, 2 cups onions, garnish.

videos.description:
This week's episode of Today's Gourmet with Jacques Pépin features two tasty seafood recipes beginning with steamed mussels marinated in fruity white wine, garlic, shallot. and fennel. Be sure not to overcook the fennel so there's a little crunch to it. For the main course, Jacques takes an ugly fish and makes a beautiful meal. Try this monkfish stuffed with cauliflower, mushroom, and broccoli rabe, served with a red sauce made with wine (yes, more wine), fish juice, tomato, and tarragon. Jacques rounds out the meal with a side dish of steamed cauliflower with chives and a cranberry souffle for dessert.

videos.description:
This week's episode of Today's Gourmet with Jacques Pépin features a hearty chili con carne with just enough heat. This recipe may become your go-to over the chili con carne featured in Jacques' Cooking at Home episode. 

He kicks off this eclectic menu with a cute melon ball bowl with port wine and coarse cracked pepper. He also makes a great case for incorporating tarragon into your salad recipes. For dessert, Jacques prepares baked apple rings with ice cream (yum!). 

Along the way, Jacques shares kitchen tips and tricks, including how to cut apple rings, how to make a bowl using cantaloupe rind, how to stop from crying when you're cutting onions (it's easier than you think), and so much more.

videos.description:
Vegetable recipes take center stage In this week's episode of Today's Gourmet with Jacques Pépin. Skip the white flour for Jacques' delightfully savory potato galette made with garlicky spinach and chives. Instead of a traditional pasta course, Jacques creates a mushroom-stuffed wonton in red wine sauce. For dessert, Jacques' favorite sous chef makes a surprise appearance: his daughter Claudine Pepin. Together, father and daughter construct an impressive caramel cage with sugar-coated edible flowers, mint leaves, basil and ice cream.

Along the way, Jacques shares kitchen tips and tricks, including how to make wontons, how to construct a dish out of caramel, and a quick way to slice scalloped potatoes.

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Artichoke with Ravigote Sauce | KQED
videos.description:
In this week's episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pépin prepares an elegant and innovative meal, beginning with artichokes with sauce ravigote, a classic acidic French sauce. For the main course, Jacques prepares a tender calf's liver filet served on a bed of sautéed red chard with caper sauce. This episode's dessert is a spectacular oeufs a la neige, a floating island dessert recipe made with egg whites, caramel, and a peach sauce. You know Jacques means business with this dessert, as he lines his kitchen floor with towels and newspaper to demonstrate how to make caramel "angel hair", a fabulous spun sugar technique that will leave your guests "ooh"ing and "aah"ing.

As usual, Jacques shares a plethora of valuable kitchen tips and tricks, including different ways to cut and serve artichoke, why you should mix beeswax in with your caramel, and the benefits of giving liver a chance.

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Eclectic Winter Recipes | KQED
videos.description:
In this week's episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pépin prepares a winter meal, though he steers away from beef stew or other hearty hibernation food you would typically expect. Instead, he starts by showing us how to scramble eggs "the French way" for his first course of smoked trout with scrambled eggs on toast. For the main course, Jacques prepares a mussel stew marinated in white wine and served with garlicky spinach and lima beans, a simple soup made with sour cream and bean broth, and ends the meal with a baked apple recipe that would work as either breakfast or dessert.

Along the way, Jacques shares a variety of kitchen tips including why you should whisk your eggs, how to tell if a mussel is fresh, how to debone trout, how to cut scallions, how to core an apple (without cutting yourself), and more.

In This Episode:
00:00: How to make scrambled eggs "the French way"
1:44 Smoked trout and scrambled eggs on toast
4:38 Mussels marinated in white wine
5:08 How to clean mussels
15:09 Simple soup recipe
16:33 How to core an apple

videos.title: Cozy Soup and Homemade Bread from Jacques Pépin | KQED
videos.description:
Carb fans, you are in for a real treat in this week's episode of Today's Gourmet. Jacques Pépin prepares garbure, a cozy  French stew made with ham, navy beans, and a host of hearty vegetables including leek, potato, cabbage, and parsnip. The soup is wonderful, but most of this episode is dedicated to the art of homemade bread. As usual, Jacques not only tells us how to do something, but why. Along the way, you'll learn which flour to use for which type of bread, how to make a starter, the best way to shape dough, and more. 

For dessert, Jacques prepares a light fruit dish with pureed cantaloupe, Grand Marnier, honey, and pineapple.

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Bavarian Cream Cake  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin prepares an innovative menu, starting with a healthy pozole with calamari rings. For dessert, Jacques makes a stunning farina Bavarian cream cake. Farina is a type of grain and can be substituted in this recipe for couscous or Cream of Wheat. The main course is veal chops with braised lettuce and shaved olives. Try something a little different for dinner tonight.

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Date Night Steak Dinner 🥩  | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin prepares a perfect date night dinner with breaded oysters and marinated flank steak served on a bed of caramelized mushrooms and shallots. The oysters are poached, topped with herbed bread crumbs, and seasoned with oyster and chili sauce. The flank steak is marinated and barbecued before being roasted in the oven. Don't miss Jacques' secret to cooking a juicy steak. For dessert, Jacques prepares a simple raspberry slush garnished with mint and served in a frosted glass.

As usual, this episode is packed with fantastic kitchen tips including using dried herb stems, how to properly cook mushrooms (hint: it takes longer than you think), and the secret to a juicy steak.

videos.title: Get Cozy with Jacques Pépin's Fall Recipes | KQED
videos.description:
Jacques Pépin makes a delicious Fall meal featuring venison steak, hearty root vegetables, and saltwater codfish. For the first course, Jacques sautés salt cod with zucchini, tomato, and olives in a white wine and tarragon sauce. The main course is a gorgeous cut of venison, served with pan-cooked sweet potatoes and a dark, rich sweet and sour sauce with red pepper, grapefruit, and peach relish. For dessert, Jacques prepares a simple fruit salad of plum and mango.

As usual, this episode is packed with fantastic kitchen tips including how to debone fish, why you should peel peppers and how to do it, when you should salt meat, how to pan-cook sweet potatoes, and more.

About Yan Can Cook:
After receiving his formal restaurant training in Hong Kong, Chef Martin Yan immigrated to Calgary, Canada where he was asked to appear in a daytime news program to demonstrate Chinese cooking. The rest, as they say, is television history. In 1978, he launched the groundbreaking Chinese cooking series 'Yan Can Cook' on public television. Infused with Martin's signature humor and energy, Yan Can Cook has gone on to become a global phenomenon and has won multiple James Beard Awards. 

See what Martin is up to now on his website: https://yancancook.com/home/
Discover more fun with food on KQED: https://www.kqed.org/food

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Inexpensive Steak Recipe | KQED
videos.description:
Are you a master of leftovers? In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pépin makes a delicious meal using ingredients he has on hand. He starts with a rustic and hearty pea soup made with leek and potato and other ingredients from his garden. For the main course, Jacques makes a classic grilled triangle steak using a "Butcher's cut" and served with peas, carrots, and herbed butter. For dessert, try his stewed plum and pistachio in filo recipe. 

This episode is packed with kitchen tips including how to pick an inexpensive cut of steak, how to make flavored "maitre'd" butter, how to shell peas, and why frozen peas are okay in Jacques' book.

In This Episode:

16:35 Stewed plums with pistachio in filo dough recipe

In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pépin makes a wonderful menu inspired by North African flavors. The star of the show is couscous with harissa and lamb and an abundance of vegetables. This recipe is more time-consuming than others, but the end result is worth it. He also makes a cured salmon gravlax, and recommends a strong wine with dinner along with an assortment of fresh and dried fruit for dessert. Happy Cooking!

In This Episode:
00:00 How to make couscous with leg of lamb
3:42 How to make harissa 
6:22 How to cook couscous 
10:34 How to season and cure salmon gravlax

videos.title: Delicious Coriander Chicken Thighs from Jacques Pépin | KQED
videos.description:
In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pépin makes a Russian feast inspired by his time spent at New York's iconic Russian Tea Room. He starts off with a delicious plate of chicken thighs cooked with coriander, white wine, onions, and cilantro. Next he moves on to a poached cod fish filet served in cream sauce with olives and capers. For dessert, Jacques prepares a Russian specialty called kissel. His version uses cranberry puree and orange zest, garnished with mint. 

In This Episode:
00:00 How to make chicken in coriander sauce
9:22 Poached cod with cream sauce recipe
11:13 Jacques Pepin discusses the merits of various pots
16:47 How to make Russian kissel with cranberry puree.

videos.title: Jacques Pepin's Pork with Red Beans and Rice | KQED
videos.description:
In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pepin prepares a wonderful menu inspired by his wife's Caribbean roots. The star of this show is a Puerto Rican pork with red beans and rice. This is a flavorful and spicy dish. The beans are marinated with cilantro, jalapeno, fresh vegetables, and pork. The rice is flavored with orange rind, hot pepper flakes, and annatto seed (to give it a rich yellow color). 

Jacques makes two different soups in this episode. One is an easy noodle soup that's ready in about 10 minutes. The other is a red bean soup using the leftovers from the main course.

To round out the meal, Jacques recreates one of his wife's childhood favorites: guava paste with mint and cream cheese on toast. This can be served as a snack or a dessert.

In This Episode:
00:00
1:00 How to make a 10-minute noodle soup
1:41 How to make Puerto Rican pork and beans
7:10 How to make red beans and rice. Jacques prepares white rice with orange rind, hot pepper flakes, onion, and annatto seed.
13:59 Red bean soup recipe
17:45 Guava paste dessert recipe

videos.title: Jacques Pepin's Vietnamese Pho Soup Will Give You Life | KQED
videos.description:
In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pepin prepares a wonderful Vietnamese menu featuring pho noodle soup. The nourishing broth is made with oxtail, beef bone, and beef shank and it is served with rice noodles colorful vegetables, spices, and garnish. Though the soup is hearty enough to be a meal in itself, Jacques also makes a fresh and crunchy savoy cabbage salad. For dessert, he whips up four beautiful banana tarts with cream and apricot jam.

Jacques throws us a curve ball at the end of this episode and shuns his usual beverage choice.

In This Episode:

videos.title: This Wacky Tricycle Race is so San Francisco | KQED Archives
videos.description:
Remember the San Francisco Tricycle Race? Between 1972 and 1993, the Mint's Memorial Day Tricycle Race in San Francisco raised thousands of dollars for both AIDS and guide dog-related charities. The concept for the race was dreamed up by Mint employee Les Balmain and Mint owner Charlotte Coleman. The colorful and chaotic race involved scores of two-person teams—sometimes in costume, sometimes in drag, sometimes in almost nothing at all—racing between bars all over the city. One person sat on the tricycle while the other pushed, and wherever the duos landed en route, they got free drinks.

Read more about wacky races in San Francisco on KQED: https://www.kqed.org/arts/13911589/sfmoma-soapbox-derby-san-francisco-wacky-races-pops-mint-parkside-tricycle-mclaren-park
#kqed #sanfrancisco #bikerace #wackyraces

videos.title: Jacques Pépin's Fluffy Omelette is a Perfect Breakfast Treat | KQED
videos.description:
In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pepin prepares a few of his all-time favorite dishes, including omelettes, breakfast potatoes, salad, and freshly baked baguette. 
He begins the episode by recreating his favorite childhood afterschool treat: a home baked baguette served with roasted nuts, grapes, and dark chocolate. Next he makes thinly-sliced home fried potatoes and a fluffy classic French omelette made with five eggs. Jacques recommends using eggs from a farmer's market and prepared with wonderful fresh herbs whenever possible. 

Dr. Curtis Ellison from Boston University joins Jacques at the table to enjoy a meal and discuss heart healthy food choices.

In This Episode:

1:00 Jacques Pepin teaches us how to make homemade baguettes
9:36 How to make scalloped potatoes
11:06 Country salad with saucisson and vinaigrette
15:37 How to make Jacques Pepin's 5-egg French omelette

videos.title: Recreate Bistro Recipes at Home with Jacques Pépin | KQED
videos.description:
Recreate an evening out at a cozy French cafe with this menu prepared by Jacques Pepin. He starts off the meal with a fennel and pear salad with sun dried tomatoes. Instead of croutons, he bakes won ton chips to give the dish an added crunch. Next he moves on to the main course: a succulent fillet of pork served with handmade gnocchi. And for dessert, forget the frapuccino, Jacques makes a frozen coffee dessert you can enjoy at home.

videos.title: Jacques Pepin's Eggplant and Brie Terrine is a Slice of Heaven  | KQED
videos.description:
In this episode of Today's Gourmet, Jacques Pepin prepares a menu derived from the South of France and it is especially easy on the eyes. He starts off with a terrine, a fantastic dish using layers of eggplant, red pepper, and brie molded into a loaf and cut into slices so you can view the gorgeous strata. Next he prepares a fresh and healthy wine-poached red snapper with leeks and cucumber garnish. He rounds out the meal with an elegant "omelet" soufflé dessert, similar to Baked Alaska. You may be intimidated to attempt a soufflé, but Jacques is here to walk you through it, step by step. Happy cooking!

In This Episode:
00:00 Jacques Pepin prepares a menu inspired by the South of France
1:00 How to make a delicious eggplant and brie terrine recipe
7:15 How to peel red peppers
9:06 How to poach fish for Jacques' red snapper poached with wine and leeks and cucumber garnish
16:06 How to make an elegant omelet soufflé, similar to baked Alaska
